steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Let w = width (cm). The length is 42 cm greater than the width, so length = w + 42.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: Area of a rectangle = length × width = (w + 42)(w) = w(w + 42).

choiceElimination:
• Choice A — $A=(w)(w+42)$: Width is w, length is w + 42; their product gives the correct area formula. ✓
  • Choice B — $A=(w)(42w)$: This would mean length = 42w (42 times the width), not 42 more than the width. ✗
  • Choice C — $A=(w)(\frac{w}{42})$: This makes length a fraction of the width, contradicting "42 cm greater." ✗
  • Choice D — $A=(w)(w-42)$: This subtracts 42 instead of adding it, reversing the relationship. ✗
That matches Choice A — $A=(w)(w+42)$.
